name: Step-1
note: StepFun's debut 100B+-parameter bilingual LLM, publicly launched March 2024; offered via API in 8K/32K/128K/256K context variants (step-1-8k/32k/128k/256k per StepFun's model list docs), since superseded by the Step-3.x series and dropped from current pricing.
verificationNotes: Existence and March 2024 launch confirmed via Wikipedia/AI Wiki/nextomoro descriptions of StepFun's 100B+-parameter Step-1. Confirmed step-1-8k/32k/128k/256k context variants genuinely exist in StepFun's official API model-list docs. No result rows were provided to verify. paramsB/date/openness/license left unchanged as they matched independent descriptions. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): Final, methodologically-diversified attempt for StepFun's Step-1 (100B+, closed API model, corpus currently has zero results) — still found nothing citable. Read the existing corpus JSON first (corpus/models/stepfun/step-1.json): prior verificationNotes confirmed existence/launch details but no result rows were ever sourced. This round widened to Chinese-language sources since StepFun is a Chinese vendor: (1) Chinese tech press — 36Kr (36kr.com/p/2706455840962441), GeekPark (geekpark.net/news/332970 and /343476), QbitAI/量子位 (qbitai.com, multiple articles), hub.baai.ac.cn, TMTPost, Zhihu, Sina Finance, CSDN, CLS.cn — all repeat only the same qualitative marketing claim ('Step-1的综合性能全面超越GPT-3.5' / surpasses GPT-3.5 in reasoning, Chinese knowledge, English knowledge, math, and code) with zero accompanying numeric scores or benchmark tables. (2) Chinese leaderboards — SuperCLUE (cluebenchmarks.com, superclueai.com, and the CLUEbenchmark/SuperCLUE GitHub README, fetched directly and checked for 'Step-1'/'阶跃星辰'/'StepFun' with zero hits) and OpenCompass/司南 (rank.opencompass.org.cn) — both only track StepFun's later multimodal models (Step-1V, Step-1o, Step-2, Step-3.x), never the original text-only Step-1. (3) Wikipedia (both zh and the en version cited in existing specSources) — confirms the launch narrative but states no benchmark scores are given for Step-1 specifically. (4) Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/providers/stepfun) — only tracks Step 3.7 Flash, Step 3.5 Flash 2603, and Step 3.5 Flash; Step-1 is absent. (5) llm-stats.com/organizations/stepfun — only lists Step-3.5-Flash and Step3-VL-10B; no Step-1. (6) LMArena/Chatbot Arena — no Step-1 entry found via search (StepFun's arena presence, where it exists, is under later model names). (7) Hugging Face — Step-1 was never open-sourced (API-only, closed license per corpus), so no HF model card exists to carry eval numbers, consistent with corpus's apiAvailable/openness fields. (8) Wayback Machine — the WebFetch tool in this environment explicitly refuses web.archive.org URLs ('Claude Code is unable to fetch from web.archive.org'), so I could not pull the vendor's original 2023/2024 launch-page snapshot directly; this is a tooling limitation, not a search dead-end I chose to skip. Grokipedia's StepFun page returned HTTP 403 and could not be read. Conclusion: this looks like a genuine no-data case — StepFun publicized Step-1's launch (Aug 2023 initial training completion per Chinese press, formal Mar 2024 platform launch) with only a vague 'beats GPT-3.5' marketing claim, and it appears no standardized benchmark table (MMLU/C-Eval/CMMLU/GSM8K/HumanEval or similar) was ever published for this specific model, likely because StepFun shifted press and leaderboard attention almost immediately to Step-1V/Step-2/Step-3.x, which do have plenty of tracked scores. Recommend the corpus keep results: [] for Step-1 going forward unless a Wayback-preserved launch page can be retrieved through a different tool with archive.org access.
